title: Valuated Delta Matroids and Principal Minors
speaker: Cynthia Vinzant
abstract: Delta matroids are a generalization of matroids, encompassing combinatorial structures such as matchings of graphs and principal minors of symmetric matrices. In this talk, I will discuss a notion of valuated Delta matroids and their representability by the principal minors of Hermitian matrices over valued fields. This generalizes work of Rincón on even Delta matroids and skew symmetric matrices. This talk is based on joint work with Nathan Cheung, Tracy Chin, and Gaku Liu.

proof_stages.label: Classical valuated-matroid benchmark
proof_stages.summary: The talk recalls valuated matroids as functions on fixed-size subsets satisfying tropical basis exchange, equivalently tropical Plücker/subdivision conditions, with representability by valuations of ordinary matrix minors.

proof_stages.label: Delta matroids, principal minors, and exchange obstruction
proof_stages.summary: General Delta matroids are introduced through symmetric exchange and cube-edge directions, principal-minor supports of symmetric or skew-symmetric matrices are shown as natural sources, and the failure of strong exchange outside the even case motivates a different valuated definition.

proof_stages.label: Subdivision definition of valuated Delta matroids
proof_stages.summary: The chosen notion is a function on the cube whose support and every cell of the induced regular subdivision have only Delta-matroid edge directions, so the cells are Delta-matroid polytopes.

proof_stages.label: Three-element structure and non-equidimensionality
proof_stages.summary: For n=2 every height function works, while for n=3 the speaker gives an explicit six-term minimum condition and observes full-dimensional as well as lower-dimensional maximal cones.

proof_stages.label: Four-element antipodal-edge obstruction
proof_stages.summary: For n=4, restrictions to three-faces must be three-element valuated Delta matroids and a large tropical minimum over minimal convex representations of the cube center must be attained twice, preventing antipodal long edges in the lower hull.

proof_stages.label: Four-face local-to-global criterion
proof_stages.summary: For arbitrary n, a function is a valuated Delta matroid precisely when its support is a Delta matroid and every four-dimensional face of the n-cube restricts to the four-element theory.

proof_stages.label: Principal-minor representability
proof_stages.summary: Skew-symmetric and symmetric principal minors give natural representations, but symmetric matrices already miss much of the n=3 fan because of the hyperdeterminantal relation, whereas Hermitian principal minors are valuated by stability, realize all n=3 cases, and still fail to fill the n=4 Dressian.

examples.name: A 2-by-4 valuated matroid with one lifted minor
examples.note: A concrete 2-by-4 Puiseux-series matrix has all 2-minor valuations zero except the {3,4} minor, illustrating how a valuated matroid produces a matroidal subdivision of the rank-2 hypersimplex/octahedron.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker computes the valuations of the 2-by-2 minors of a small 2-by-4 matrix and then traces the induced regular subdivision of the octahedron with one vertex lifted

examples.name: Three-element Delta matroid with no singleton bases
examples.note: The Delta matroid consisting of the empty set, all even two-subsets, and {1,2,3} illustrates the new cube-edge directions and is used as the visible counterexample showing why strong exchange cannot define the desired general theory.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker constructs the three-element delta matroid, checks its polytope edge directions, realizes it via principal minors, and uses it to verify failure of strong exchange

examples.name: Parity height function on the 3-cube
examples.note: Assigning valuation zero to even subsets and one to odd subsets gives a 3-cube subdivision whose triangulation reveals the full-dimensional cone phenomenon and later marks a cone missed by symmetric principal-minor realizations.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker assigns parity heights on the 3-cube and works through the resulting lifted subdivision/triangulation and its role in the n=3 condition
